repo_prior_art.cite: pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope_v1 — binary-IV capped-envelope geometry, not a multivalued quotient section.

published_prior_art.cite: BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 uses M-equivalence/shift normalization; exact Appendix D.1 comparison requires source verification because the primary PDF was inaccessible.
BalkePearl1997 — finite response-type LP gives sharp IV bounds; SongGuoChanRichardson2024, pp. 4–5, computes categorical-IV linear-function bounds by LP.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope_v1 — binary-IV capped-envelope result, with no unrestricted three-arm vertex catalogue.

published_prior_art.cite: No inspected source supplies this displayed gauge-fixed witness; BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 Theorem 4 family comparison remains pending primary-source verification.
No inspected published multivalued exact-signature recognizer; the claimed binary-only Boushehrian comparison requires primary-source verification.

AvisFukuda1996 gives generic reverse-search architecture; KhachiyanBorosBorysElbassioniGurvich2008 establishes hardness for general unbounded H-polyhedra, neither subsuming an IV-specific implicit-system enumerator.
No inspected source gives a regime-wide strict-incompleteness result; the exact Boushehrian family-size comparison awaits primary-source verification.

soundness_flags.label: Observed-law class and all endpoint statements
soundness_flags.one_line: The core defines p as the conditional cells P(Y=y,D=d|Z=z), so every arm sums to one and 1^T A_{n,l}q=l, but def:iv-law-class instead requires p∈Δ(I_{n,l}), whose total mass is one; for l≥2 this makes P^IV_{n,l} empty and vacates the endpoint claim.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives necessary, sufficient, non-redundant categorical-IV inequalities and states that bounds on linear functionals of the joint counterfactual distribution are computed by linear programming; it also gives a convex-program finite-sample coverage construction.
The article states that it establishes nonparametric, tight bounds on the average treatment effect under random assignment with imperfect compliance, using linear programming.
The cited article exists as David Avis and Komei Fukuda, Reverse Search for Enumeration, Discrete Applied Mathematics 65, 21–46 (1996).

source_verification_receipts.version: Discrete and Computational Geometry 39 (2008), article PDF mirror
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article states hardness of generating all vertices of a feasible inequality-presented polyhedron, while distinguishing the bounded case.
The article develops inference for intersection bounds defined by an infimum or supremum, equivalently values of linear programs with potentially infinite constraint sets.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace p∈Δ(I_{n,l}) everywhere by the product conditional-law simplex {p≥0: Σ_{y,d}p_{y,d,z}=1 for each z}; then recheck the primal constraints, endpoint functional, and every stated sanity reduction.

Inspect and cite the exact arXiv:2604.12802v1 theorem/appendix pages before retaining the BBAK formula, lower-to-upper dual conversion, binary-completeness claim, and omitted-family conclusion; the primary PDF was unavailable to this review.
Strengthen the deficit program from an isolated (2,3) certificate to a lifting or perturbation-stable infinite regime of omitted vertices, with an explicit embedding from the three-arm witness.

recommended_next_step: Revise the conditional-law normalization first, then complete primary-source verification of Boushehrian et al.; the field-tier recognition/enumeration kernel can proceed once those corrections hold.
